title: Velvety Ginger German Cabbage Gratin
description: A fermented, velvety German gratin that blends tangy sauerkraut, spicy ginger, and creamy yogurt for a sugar‑free, meal‑prep friendly dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200 °C (400 °F). Grease a 23 × 33 cm baking dish with a little olive oil.
Wash the cabbage and cut into 8 wedges. Blanch the wedges in boiling salted water for 2 min, then drain and set aside.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the grated ginger and sauté for 30 s until fragrant.
Stir in the yogurt, tomato paste, mustard powder, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook for 2 min until the mixture thickens slightly.
Fold in the drained sauerkraut and sauté for another 2 min, letting the flavors mingle. Remove from heat.
Arrange the blanched cabbage wedges in a single layer in the prepared dish. Spoon the ginger sauce over each wedge, covering completely.
Sprinkle the shredded mozzarella evenly over the top of the gratin.
Cover the dish with foil and bake for 20 min.
Remove the foil and return the gratin to the oven uncovered for 8–10 min, or until the cheese is golden and bubbly.
Let the gratin rest for 5 min before serving. Garnish with fresh dill if desired.
